title: Vantara AI Slapped With Preliminary Data Fine, Sources Say
body: Two people with knowledge of the matter told EuroPrivacy Wire that Ireland's data protection authority has already determined that Vantara AI violated EU data transfer rules and is preparing to issue a formal decision — a step beyond what the DPC described publicly on Monday. The regulator's statement characterized the matter as an open inquiry, but one source, speaking on condition of anonymity because the process is confidential, said a draft decision has been circulated internally. A second source disputed that characterization, saying no draft decision exists and that the inquiry is genuinely in early stages. Vantara AI did not respond to questions about a potential fine. The company recently announced plans to expand its European engineering team, a move observers noted would be complicated by a significant regulatory penalty. DataWatch EU, the group whose complaints triggered the probe, published a blog post Wednesday calling on the DPC to act "without further delay." Vantara's Stockholm offices declined to comment on the internal process.
